prompt:
Perf ticket, retail-audit tooling. tally.ts renders the fixed-width tally sheets our auditors print — sections plus 44-column rows, exact layout documented in the module header. It was written for single-aisle spot checks and it shows: the full-store export (150k rows) went from 'fine' to 'kill it after ten minutes' as stores onboarded. The builder grows one big string and re-copies everything it has already built on every line it appends — textbook quadratic append. I sprinkled the onCopy accounting hook through it last sprint while chasing this, and the numbers confirm it: reported copy volume explodes with row count while the finished sheet is only ~6.7MB.

Make the builder linear-ish without changing a byte of output. The layout contract is pinned and must not move: section lines, column widths, truncation and padding rules, the wider-numeral exception for qty, render() idempotent and callable between appends. Keep the SheetBuilder / SheetOpts API and keep the copy accounting honest — onCopy(result.length) must still fire for every string the builder materializes while assembling sheet text (padding, concatenation, joining, or whatever equivalent you end up with; slices and number-to-string conversions don't count). The perf suite budgets the cumulative reported total at 6x the final sheet length for the 150k-row export, so anything that keeps re-copying its buffer can't pass.

node --test test_tally.ts is the acceptance contract — don't touch the test file.
